metadata.summary: Select the right text embedding model by use case, constraint, and provider: proprietary vs open-source, latency vs quality trade-offs, Matryoshka and quantization support.

content.title: Text Embedding Model Selection
content.summary: Choose an embedding model by matching use case (RAG, search, clustering, classification), constraints (budget, latency, privacy, language), and provider capabilities (Matryoshka dimensions, quantization, asymmetric input types). MTEB scores are general — always benchmark on your own data before committing.

why.p: Text embeddings are dense vector representations that capture semantic meaning. Similar texts have vectors that are close together in the embedding space, enabling semantic search, RAG, clustering, classification, and anomaly detection. The wrong model for a use case costs 3-8% retrieval quality or 10x unnecessary spend — choosing correctly upfront avoids full re-embedding of large corpora.

list.item: Starting a new semantic search or RAG project and need to pick an embedding provider.
Evaluating whether to switch from one embedding model to another.
Choosing between cloud API and local/self-hosted models.
Optimizing an existing system for cost, latency, or quality.
Exact keyword or structured data lookup — BM25 or SQL is faster and more precise.
Tiny corpora under 500 docs — cosine similarity across all docs is trivially fast without indexing.
When the domain is highly specialized and no domain-adapted model exists — consider fine-tuning or BM25 fallback first.
Real-time streaming data where batch embedding latency blocks the pipeline.
text-embedding-3-large (OpenAI) — 256-3072 dims, 8191 token max, $0.13/1M tokens, MTEB 64.6%. Best for production RAG, high quality.
text-embedding-3-small (OpenAI) — 256-1536 dims, 8191 token max, $0.02/1M tokens, MTEB 62.3%. Best for cost-sensitive apps.
voyage-4-large (Voyage AI) — 256-2048 dims, 32K token max, $0.12/1M tokens, MTEB ~68%. SOTA retrieval, MoE architecture.
voyage-3.5 (Voyage AI) — 256-2048 dims, 32K token max, $0.06/1M tokens, MTEB ~66%. Best price/quality ratio.
voyage-context-3 (Voyage AI) — 1024 dims, 32K token max, $0.06/1M tokens. Designed for contextualized chunk embedding.
embed-v4 (Cohere) — 256-1536 dims, 128K token max, $0.10/1M tokens, MTEB ~65%. Multimodal (images + text), long documents.
embed-multilingual-v3.0 (Cohere) — 1024 dims, 512 token max, $0.10/1M tokens. 100+ languages.
mistral-embed (Mistral) — 1024 dims, 8192 token max, $0.10/1M tokens. 77.8% accuracy (not MTEB average).
BGE-M3 (BAAI) — 1024 dims, 8192 token max, MTEB 68.1%, slow. Best quality, supports hybrid search (dense + sparse).
Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B — 1024 dims, 8192 token max, MTEB ~67%, medium speed. Instruction-aware.
nomic-embed-text-v2 — 768 dims, 8192 token max, MTEB ~66%, fast. Long context, multilingual.
bge-large-en-v1.5 (BAAI) — 1024 dims, 512 token max, MTEB 63.6%, medium. Balanced quality/speed.
e5-large-v2 (Microsoft) — 1024 dims, 512 token max, MTEB ~64%, medium. Semantic similarity.
all-MiniLM-L6-v2 — 384 dims, 256 token max, MTEB ~58%, very fast. Real-time, sub-10ms latency.
EmbeddingGemma-300M — 768 dims, 8192 token max, MTEB ~62%, fast. On-device deployment.
Production RAG: voyage-3.5 (primary), text-embedding-3-large (alternative). Best retrieval quality.
Cost-sensitive: text-embedding-3-small (primary), BGE-M3 local (alternative). Low cost, good quality.
Multilingual: embed-v4 (primary), BGE-M3 (alternative). 100+ languages.
Long documents: voyage-context-3 (primary), embed-v4 128K (alternative). Document-level context.
Code search: voyage-code-3 (primary), CodeBERT (alternative). Code-specific training.
Air-gapped/Private: BGE-M3 (primary), nomic-embed-text-v2 (alternative). No API calls.
Real-time search: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (primary), nomic-embed-text-v2 (alternative). Sub-10ms latency.
Multimodal: embed-v4 (primary), CLIP (alternative). Images + text.
Legal/Finance: voyage-3-large (primary), voyage-finance-2 (alternative). Domain-specific.
Budget under $100/month: text-embedding-3-small + caching.
Latency under 10ms: local model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2).
No external APIs: BGE-M3 or nomic-embed-text-v2.
GPU available: BGE-M3 with FP16.
CPU only: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 or Model2Vec.
Massive scale (100M+ docs): quantized voyage-3.5 (int8).

section.name: feature-comparison

list.item: text-embedding-3-large: supports 256/512/1024/1536/3072 dims, ~4% quality loss at 256d.
voyage-3.5: supports 256/512/1024/2048 dims, ~3% quality loss at 256d.
embed-v4: supports 256/512/1024/1536 dims, ~3% quality loss at 256d.
nomic-embed-text-v2: supports 64/128/256/512/768 dims, ~5% quality loss at 256d.
voyage-3.5: float32/float16/int8/binary. INT8 reduces storage 4x with less than 1% quality loss.
embed-v4: float32/float16/int8. No binary.
text-embedding-3-large: float32 only. No quantization, no local option.
BGE-M3: float32/float16/int8/binary.

section.p: Asymmetric input types (query vs document differentiation):

list.item: Cohere: search_document, search_query, classification, clustering.
Voyage AI: document, query.
OpenAI: no input types. Uses symmetric embeddings only.

section.name: decision-framework
section.p: Decision framework — follow these branches in order:

list.item: Need multimodal (images)? Yes: embed-v4 (Cohere).
Need more than 8K token context? Yes: embed-v4 (128K) or voyage-context-3.
Cannot use external API? Yes: BGE-M3 (quality) or all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (speed).
Budget above $500/month? No: text-embedding-3-small + caching.
Need SOTA retrieval? Yes: voyage-3.5 or voyage-4-large. No: text-embedding-3-large.

section.name: provider-notes
section.p: OpenAI: Simple API, good quality, native dimension reduction. No input types, no quantization, no local option. Batching up to 2048 texts per request. Best models: text-embedding-3-large (quality), text-embedding-3-small (cost).

Voyage AI (MongoDB): SOTA quality, shared embedding space, MoE architecture. Newer ecosystem, limited enterprise integrations. Special voyage-context-3 for contextualized chunks. Best models: voyage-4-large (quality), voyage-3.5 (balanced).
Cohere: Multimodal (embed-v4), 128K context, mature API. Higher latency, complex pricing. Input types for asymmetric search. Best models: embed-v4 (multimodal), embed-multilingual-v3.0 (languages).

Local (sentence-transformers): Free, private, customizable, GPU acceleration. Requires infrastructure, slower than APIs usually. 15,000+ pre-trained models on HuggingFace. Best models: BGE-M3 (quality), all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (speed).

rule.statement: Always benchmark candidates on YOUR domain data before committing to a model. MTEB scores measure general retrieval; domain-specific corpora (legal, medical, code, enterprise) diverge significantly from MTEB results.
rule.rationale: MTEB benchmark scores are general benchmarks. A model ranking lower on MTEB may outperform a higher-ranked model on your specific data because the training distribution aligns better. Always measure Recall@10, MRR, and Precision@K on 100-500 real query-document pairs from your corpus before choosing.

rule.statement: When using Cohere or Voyage, always set input_type: use "document"/"search_document" for indexing and "query"/"search_query" for retrieval. Omitting input_type on query embedding drops retrieval quality by 3-8%.
rule.rationale: Asymmetric embedding (different representations for documents and queries) measurably improves retrieval precision because queries and documents have different linguistic structures. This is not supported by OpenAI — if asymmetric search matters, pick Cohere or Voyage.

antipattern.description: Choosing the top MTEB model without testing on domain data, then discovering 10% recall gap in production.
antipattern.reason: MTEB measures average retrieval quality across general domains. Domain shift (legal, financial, scientific, code) can reverse model rankings entirely. Benchmark first, always.

antipattern.description: Embedding drift: switching embedding model or updating model version without re-embedding the entire corpus.
antipattern.reason: Embedding spaces are model-specific. Mixed-model vector stores produce unpredictable similarity scores because the geometric relationships between vectors differ. No partial migration is possible — re-embed everything or keep the old model.
